questions.question: Explain the three types of stations in HDLC with their roles.
keyPoints: Primary station - Controls link operations, initiates communication, polls secondaries
Secondary station - Responds to primary commands, cannot initiate transmission
Combined station - Functions as both primary and secondary, used in balanced mode

questions.question: Describe the bit stuffing and destuffing process in HDLC.
keyPoints: Insert '0' after five consecutive '1' bits in data to prevent flag pattern
Ensures data transparency - flag sequence doesn't appear in data
Receiver removes '0' after five consecutive '1' bits - destuffing

questions.question: List and explain the functions of S-frames in HDLC.
keyPoints: RR (Receive Ready) - Positive acknowledgment, ready to receive next frame
RNR (Receive Not Ready) - Negative acknowledgment, cannot receive more frames
REJ (Reject) - Go-back-N error recovery, request retransmission from specific frame

questions.question: Compare Normal Response Mode (NRM) and Asynchronous Balanced Mode (ABM).
keyPoints: NRM: Point-to-multipoint, primary polls secondary, secondary can only transmit when polled
ABM: Point-to-point, both stations are equal peers, can initiate without polling
ABM is more efficient for dedicated links, NRM for multipoint configurations

questions.question: Draw and explain the HDLC frame structure.
keyPoints: Flag (8 bits) - 01111110 marks start and end
Address (8 bits) - identifies secondary station
Control (8/16 bits) - frame type and sequence numbers
Information (variable) - user data (only in I-frames)
FCS (16 bits) - error detection using CRC
Flag (8 bits) - closing flag

questions.question: Explain the role of P/F (Poll/Final) bit in HDLC.
keyPoints: P (Poll) bit set by primary to solicit response from secondary
F (Final) bit set by secondary to indicate end of its response
P=1 in command frame requires response with F=1
Used in NRM for polling secondaries

questions.question: What are U-frames in HDLC? List important U-frame commands and their purposes.
keyPoints: U-frames are Unnumbered frames for link management
SABM - Set Asynchronous Balanced Mode (connection establishment)
DISC - Disconnect (terminate connection)
UA - Unnumbered Acknowledgment (response to U-commands)
DM - Disconnected Mode (indicates cannot establish connection)
FRMR - Frame Reject (reports unrecoverable errors)
